entry.title: Re: BAPI to park document in FV50 and FV50L
entry.content:
PRELIMINARY_POSTING_FB01 .

Try using this FM and pass FV50 in importing parameter I_TCODE and also in table T_BKPF in tcode field.

entry.title: Trial SAP BO 4.3

Hello everyone !

I wanted to know if it is possible to install the trial version of SAP BO 4.3
If yes, from what link can we do it?
THANKS !

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA Public Cloud - Show country-specific referenc...
entry.content: Dear @Vijayendra, In the link you provided I can see our field listed as "Dependent on company code", however, I don't see how to enable this field in our case. Our country is "Dominican Republic", which as such is not localized by SAP, but we're using CLT to achieve the same. My question is specifically how to enable this field in our Dominican Republic Company Code...

entry.title: Re: How to resolve resnponse from a Web Service call in a...
entry.content:
Just create a custom script.

  • Input of type Any
  • Output of type Any

And then the code is just:

return myInput.inputs[0].d.results[0].QuantityInBaseUnit;

You'll of course have to bind the output of the Call Web Service to the custom script.

But all of this is if you want to store it in a variable. You don't need a custom script because you can use the expression editor to get the value you want directly (below is me using the root of Northwind service to get name of the first entity):

entry.title: Re: Static images are not displayed correctly in SAP Android preview
entry.content: This issue is solved.

entry.title: Re: Quick hacks: Displaying fields
entry.content:
😺

P.S.: Never thought of you as lazy, but good to know

entry.title: Re: ABAP: Fast JSON Serialization
entry.content: Any recent (performance-wise) breakthrough in generic JSON serialization / deserialization? 😬

entry.title: Re: "This application uses '@sap/cds' version 6.8, which is not compatible with the installed '
entry.content:
Hi Muraleedhar,

have you tried adding @sap/cds-dk @6.8 to your dev dependencies?

`yarn add @sap/cds-dk^6.8 --dev`

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP Discovery Centre - Integration Suite mission question on Trial Account
entry.content: Hi! I've checked this iflow in my trial because I've done it in the past and the URL I used and worked for me was. "https://refapp-espm-ui-cf.cfapps.eu10.hana.ondemand.com/espm-cloud-web/espm.svc" this URL still works so try this one!

entry.title: Newbie
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

My name is Hanh Phan and I’m excited to be a part of this SAP Learning Community! I’m currently working towards my SAP Certified Associate - Implementation Consultant - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with the Vower learning group, and I’m really looking forward to expanding my knowledge.I’m also open to connecting with others who are interested in supply chain management.  Looking forward to learning and growing with all of you!

entry.title: Re: How to enforce a maximum of 100 of rating in a goal p...
entry.content:
@Hermine2 

You can try below code:

<field-definition id="metric-lookup-table" type="table" required="false" detail="false" viewdefault="on" showlabel="true" default-calc-type="interpolate" field-show-coaching-advisor="false" cascade-update="push-down">
<field-label>Performance Indicators</field-label>
<field-description>Performance Indicators</field-description>
<table-row-label>Performance Indicators</table-row-label>
<table-column id="rating" type="number" required="false" cascade-update="push-down">
<column-label lang="en_US">Rating (Value)</column-label>
<column-description>Rating Value</column-description>
</table-column>
<table-column id="description" type="text" required="false" cascade-update="push-down">
<column-label>Performance Target Description</column-label>
<column-description>Write Performance Target Description here</column-description>
</table-column>
<table-column id="achievement" type="number" required="false" cascade-update="push-down">
<column-label>Target Wieght</column-label>
<column-label lang="en_US">Maximum Points</column-label>
<column-description>Key Performance Indicator</column-description>
</table-column>
</field-definition>
<field-definition id="rating" type="number" required="false" detail="false" viewdefault="off" showlabel="true" display="number" field-show-coaching-advisor="false" cascade-update="push-down">
<field-label>Score</field-label>
<field-description>Score</field-description>
</field-definition>
<field-definition id="actual-achievement" type="number" required="false" detail="false" viewdefault="off" showlabel="true" field-show-coaching-advisor="false" cascade-update="push-down">
<field-label>Actual Performance</field-label>
<field-description>Actual Performance</field-description>
</field-definition>

 

<calculator id="actualAcheivement"><![CDATA[rating]]></calculator>
<calculator id="test1"><![CDATA[(weight*Rating)]]></calculator>
<auto-population field="rating" mode="auto" calc-type="col">
<rule>
<calculated-result calculator-id="test1"/>
</rule>
</auto-population>
<auto-population field="metric-lookup-table" mode="auto" calc-type="col">
<rule>
<rule-condition/>
<table-result>
<row>
<col id="achievement"><![CDATA[0]]></col>
<col id="description"><![CDATA[Achieved 0% to 0%]]></col>
<col id="rating"><![CDATA[0]]></col>
</row>
<row>
<col id="achievement"><![CDATA[100]]></col>
<col id="description"><![CDATA[Achieved 99.5% to 100%]]></col>
<col id="rating"><![CDATA[100]]></col>
</row>
</table-result>
</rule>
</auto-population>
<auto-population field="actual-achievement" mode="auto" calc-type="col">
<rule>
<calculated-result calculator-id="actualAcheivement"/>
</rule>
</auto-population>

entry.title: Offshore vs. Onshore Instance: China Subsidiary-based Rollout for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
In today's interconnected world, Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) systems are the backbone of global business operations, seamlessly integrating key functions such as finance, supply chain management, and human resources. As companies expand their global footprint, choosing the right SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system instance strategy for the Chinese market becomes crucial for operational success.

Marco_Valencia and I aim to guide businesses in making an informed choice between offshore and onshore ERP instances for their China subsidiary-based rollout of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. We focus on critical aspects such as regulations, performance, and strategic alignment. However, given that regulations are continually evolving and may have varying implications based on the nature and sector of businesses, it is essential to seek specialized legal and technical advice to thoroughly understand Chinese regulations and decide on the best strategy. While SAP is not able to provide any legal advice, we aim to help you understand some of the strategies and implications you might encounter when making your decision.

Understanding SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition System Instances

To kick things off, let's define what SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system instances are. These are complete environments (a 3-system landscape that includes development, test, and production systems including the HANA database, etc.) where software and data are hosted and managed. When it comes to deploying these instances to cover business requirements for Chinese subsidiaries, businesses usually choose between offshore and onshore options.

Now, we will compare offshore and onshore deployments, highlighting some of the benefits and drawbacks. This will provide you with a point of reference for evaluating options to meet the requirements of your Chinese subsidiary in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Offshore Instances: Hosted in data centers located outside of China, companies often leverage existing global infrastructure to cover all group entities, including Chinese subsidiaries.

Offshore Instance – Central Single Instance (Public Cloud only) 

Figure 1 - In the example below, we show a company headquartered in the USA with two subsidiaries: one in Germany and another in China. The customer deployed a central single instance of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, following an offshore instance strategy for China & Germany based on a USA Data Center.

Benefits:

  • Cost-Effective: Significant savings by leveraging existing global IT infrastructure and economies of scale.
  • Standardization: Ensures consistent management and processes across the enterprise via corporate template-based deployment, facilitating easier cross-entity comparisons and benchmarking.
  • Seamless Governance: Centralized process ownership aids in maintaining uniformity and simplifies business process governance across entities, enabling more efficient global reporting.
  • Intercompany Process: Out-of-box configured business processes to support cross-company transactions, reducing reconciliation efforts and improving financial closing times.
  • Unified Reporting: Centralized data storage enables real-time, enterprise-wide reporting and analytics, providing a holistic view of the organization's performance with the ability to tailor reports for specific units.
  • Master Data Harmonization: Easier implementation of global data standards, ensuring consistency in master data across all entities and facilitating more accurate group-level analysis.

Drawbacks:

  • Potential Compliance Challenges: Potential changes in Chinese data residency laws can lead to legal complications or operational restrictions.
  • Potential Latency Issues: Potential latency issues can hinder real-time operations and business efficiency in China, affecting the timeliness of data for decision-making.
  • Data Transfer Restrictions: Cross-border data transfer limitations may impede real-time reporting and analytics capabilities for the Chinese operations.
  • Maintenance schedule: Offshore maintenance schedule (Upgrades, Hotfix Collections, etc.) can overlap with Chinese business hours.

Onshore Instances: Hosted within China, often through local providers, these instances are tailored specifically to meet Chinese regulations and business requirements.

Onshore Instance – Federated Strategy (Public Cloud only) 

Figure 2 - In the example below, we showcase a company headquartered in the USA, with two subsidiaries: one in Germany and another in China. The customer deployed a federated instance strategy of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, following an onshore instance strategy for China. This provides a single instance to manage the Chinese subsidiary based on a Chinese data center, while managing the rest of the countries with their one respective instance in the USA.

Benefits:

  • Regulatory Compliance: Ease of adhering to local Chinese legal requirements and data protection laws, reducing legal risks and potential penalties.
  • Enhanced Performance: Minimal latency ensures efficient system performance and user experience, enabling real-time data processing and reporting.
  • Localized Security: Better control with security measures tailored to local needs and practices, potentially reducing the risk of data breaches.
  • Operational Autonomy: Subsidiaries can operate independently, addressing specific requirements and needs efficiently, including local reporting standards.
  • Tailored Innovation: Align new innovations with localized business processes, allowing for agile adaptations to changes in the local Chinese market.
  • Tailored Analytics: Ability to develop and implement analytics tools specifically designed for the Chinese market and regulatory environment.
  • Minimized Disruption: Limited system downtime affects only impacted Chinese units, reducing disruptions to global operations and reporting. The onshore maintenance schedule, which includes upgrades and hotfix collections, is separate from other customer offshore operations.

Drawbacks:

  • Higher Costs: Increased deployment, operational and maintenance costs, including potential duplication of systems and analytics tools.
  • Integration Issues: Complexity in integrating with other SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition systems and potential cross-border data flow restrictions, complicating group-level reporting and analytics.
  • Fragmented Financial Planning: Financial consolidation might face complications due to the fragmented systems, requiring additional effort to ensure data consistency and accuracy.
  • Intercompany Process challenges: Intercompany processes may not be supported out-of-the-box, requiring additional custom integration among instances and potentially leading to reconciliation discrepancies.
  • Master Data Inconsistencies: Challenges in maintaining consistent master data across separate instances, potentially leading to discrepancies in reporting and analytics at the group level.
  • Reduced Global Visibility: Separate instances may limit real-time visibility into Chinese operations for global management, complicating decision-making and performance monitoring.

Key Considerations for Choosing Your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition System Instance

Organizational Unit Set-up

Understanding the organizational unit design versus the deployment model (offshore and onshore) is important to analyze carefully, as simplifying organizational units can also simplify intercompany processes and aid in decision-making.

Business Scale and Growth

Small to medium enterprises (SMEs) might favor offshore instances for immediate operational efficiency and cost benefits, whereas larger corporations could consider a hybrid approach. This hybrid approach leverages both onshore and offshore systems to balance costs, performance, and compliance as they scale, and to comply with changing regulations.

Geopolitical and Economic Factors

Geopolitical tensions, such as trade policies and international relations, can heavily influence the decision. Emerging trade restrictions may suddenly make offshore instances more problematic. Staying agile and well-informed on these factors ensures better strategic alignment.

Vendor Capabilities

Assessing the ERP vendor’s infrastructure and support capabilities within China is crucial. A reputable vendor with established local support can ease deployment and maintenance burdens, ensuring smoother operations. For example, technology providers like SAP support customers' compliance efforts by delivering technical features and functionalities that help adhere to applicable laws. Executing an onshore instance strategy with SAP will require a new contract with SAP China; we advise you to check with your current account executive.

Practical Steps to Making the Decision 

Assess Your Current and Future Needs Conduct a thorough assessment of your needs, encompassing current operations, compliance requirements, and future growth projections. Engage key stakeholders from various departments to capture a comprehensive view.

Consult with Experts

While SAP maintains a strong focus on China Regulatory Compliance, their information should not substitute for legal or regulatory advice. Always seek independent legal and regulatory advice tailored to your specific needs. Consult with ERP experts, including local and international consultants, for tailored advice. Their experience can uncover hidden challenges and opportunities.

Review Case Studies and Success Stories

Analyze case studies from similar businesses that have successfully managed ERP rollouts in China. These real-world examples offer valuable lessons and strategies for your decision-making process.

Conclusion

As of today, September 2024, most of our public cloud customers running operations in China currently follow the one global offshore instance approach, but several others are exploring the local onshore instance option. So choosing between offshore and onshore SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system instances for your China Subsidiary-based rollout is a multifaceted decision influenced by compliance, performance, and strategic factors. Onshore instances often offer better compliance and performance but come with higher costs and complexity. Offshore instances can be cost-effective but can face compliance and latency challenges. The best choice depends on your specific business needs, growth plans, and the geopolitical landscape. Consult with your ERP experts, run pilot tests, and thoroughly assess your requirements to create a customized solution that ensures both legal compliance and operational efficiency.

We would like to thank Jan Musil, Jens Fuchs, Khaled Musilhy , Janusz Smilek, Chi Song and Bert Schulze for their review and feedback on this blog.

Some sources for reference

Data Security Law of the People's Republic of China

Personal Information Protection Law of the People's Republic of China

China issues regulations on cross-border data flows

How to allow two affiliated companies to participate in a single intercompany process

How to transfer stock between different legal entities of a coporate group

Integration Architecture Guide for Cloud and Hybrid Landscapes

entry.title: Business Catalog missing in App "Manage Launchpad Pages"
entry.content:
Hi Experts! 

I created a RAP App on BTP and now try to create a Tile in the Launchpad. I followed tutorials and everything worked fine except that my Business Catalog doesn't appear in the App "Manage Launchpad Pages". When I assign other Catalogs to the Business Role, they appear. So, everything else seams to be correct (Assignment of Role, User, Space, Page, etc.). Does anyone know, what could be the reason I don't see my catalog?

 

Thanks and regards, Sebastian

entry.title: Re: OpenXML crushes the database
entry.content: It does also crash the database engine on 17.0.11.7821 on Windows with a Win1252 database.

entry.title: Error FWM 02151 Using SAP Business Objects 4.3. The token between Datasphere and BO expire.
entry.content:
A few months ago we connected sap datasphere with bo 4.3 directly without universes in between. For that we configure the token, which does not expire. But every now and then it happens to us that it blocks and the user cannot access the report. What is the reason and what is the permanent solution?

We provisionally found that by accessing bo with the user, we renewed the token and it works

Thanks in advance!!

Natalia.

entry.title: Re: Automating SAP DSP Task Chains with CLI Wrapping: FastAPI Integration with SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content: Anyone else get this error?  My Redirect URI is correct:CLI ErrorRedirect URI

entry.title: Enable Now - PowerBI Data Point for User Roles?
entry.content:
Hello,

I'm using PowerBI to create Enable Now reporting and would really love to be able to pull data on User Roles and users within each of those roles.

I've reviewed the guide of which data points are available and we have one for Organizational Units but not for User Roles. I've tried to modify the data point for OUs to make it pull data on User Roles but unfortunately that wasn't successful. 

Are there any secret data points that I could use, or does anybody else have an idea how I could extract the data I need from Enable Now?

Thanks so much for any ideas you may have,

Andrew

entry.title: Schedule of Fixed Assets - Global Report shows # when you add field Cost Center (Fixed Asset)
entry.content:
Why does the the Report Schedule of Fixed Assets - Global displays # in the fields Cost Center (Fixed Asset), Cost Center, Segment and Profit Center?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Schedule of Fixed Assets - Global Report shows # when...
entry.content:
The logic to fetch the values for the characteristics like, Profit center, Cost center, Cost Center (Fixed Asset) and Segment are not available in the report GLOFXAU01_Q0007 and data source GLOFXAU01.

Here, you can have the key figures and field: Cost Center (Fixed Asset), Segment, Profit center and Cost center displayed.

You can only add these fields to the report but # is displayed.

You can use other report Schedule of Fixed Assets--> Technical ID:FINFXAU01_Q0002 to show the fields information for Cost center.

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP Discovery Centre - Integration Suite mission...
entry.content:
You, sir, are a bona fide  rockstar! The link worked.

Thanks so very much!

entry.title: SAP SuccessFactors integration with SAP Digital Manufacturing system
entry.content:
Hi All,

We have a scenario where user certification need to maintained through the SAP SuccessFactors like below:

As we checked, while going through the documentation for DMC API, we found out that there is one API Overview | Certification | SAP Business Accelerator Hub that DMC can use to expose for CRUD certification operation. 

 

Please let me know if anyone has worked upon this kind of requirement where certificate need to flow through from SAP SuccessFactors to SAP DM (manage certification app) in DMC.

 

Regards,

Ankit Gupta

entry.title: First sync SAP MDK timeout
entry.content:
Hello experts!

I'm facing a problem while the app is trying to retrieve the data and creating the offline data store. This is happening because I have one entity which has 19k registers. To get a better understand,  I forced the service to provide a TOP=1000 registers and it worked very well. That's why I'm pretty confident the problem is timeout, not the odata service.

Now I have to solve the sync problem with all of those 19k registers. First, I thought SAP MDK had a timeout sync but I coudn't find it inside the services, bundle or actions.

Second, I thought SAP MDK does pagination while retrieving the data. So I traced the OData query SAP MDK did, but I didn't see any query options or params like $filter, $top, $skip. So I'm assuming is always a fullsync in this scenario.

Someone can help me to understand if I'm right in these analysis and what can I do to increase the timeout sync in SAP MDK?

I have a delta sync logic, but in this case is the first sync, a full sync to create the offline data store.

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Techtoberfest 2024
Popup Notification while using FFID & Notification to leads
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Good day.

Could you please advise your thoughts on the below items or any other suggestions. 

1) While logging in to FFIDs analysts are providing Reason codes and transaction details. 

So, can we create any popup message to make them cautious about the usage further ? 

2) I want to escalate to next stage whether there will be same controllers in the next stage , but an additional notification will trigger to managers for those logs which are not approved for more than 10 days. 

or in other words, i want to trigger notification (just notification) to leads for those logs which are not approved for more than 10 days. 

 

Please help. 

 

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Reject Supplier Quotation (internal) in S/4HANA Manage RFQs/ Manage Supplier Quotations
entry.content: Hi all, we are also facing the same problem. Is there any solution available?

entry.title: Re: ABAP Cloud Developer Trial 2022 Available Now
entry.content:
I had to restart my server and now it's stuck at "HDB: starting". 
I don't think there is any issue of memory (32GB) or disk space (70%) since there was no issue before the restart.

Which log file can I check or is there any way to make this startup process more verbose?

entry.title: SAP Commerce Cloud Q3 ‘24 Release Highlights
entry.content:
In Q3 2024, we've been hard at work to expand and enhance our unified commerce solution with new capabilities, including new AI functionality as well as core platform improvements. This will ensure that our customers operate in a secure, compliant and scalable manner, allowing them to focus their energy on their business. From zone redundancies to self-service premium disaster recovery, these backend improvements add an extra layer of stability and resilience, ensuring your commerce operations run without a hitch.

Our new AI functionalities are aimed at boosting efficiency. We've added AI Image Generation to the SAP CX AI Toolkit and announced the ChannelEngine partnership at SAP CX Live for AI-powered marketplace management. We'll delve deeper into this exciting partnership in this post.

The SAP CX Live virtual event also showcased how our comprehensive SAP Customer Experience portfolio helps your customers get more of what they want. For an in-depth look at the business advantages of using SAP CX solutions with SAP ERP, check out the new paper, Analyzing the Economic Benefits of Using AI-Powered SAP CX Solutions Natively Connected to SAP ERP.

Now, let's explore the standout features we've introduced over the past three months for SAP Commerce Cloud. Don't miss our Q3 2024 Release Highlights webcast for all the latest innovations.

 

Insightful

Merchandising your products just got easier with AI

Have you ever considered using AI to automatically create product images to show to customers? Wouldn’t that save you time and resources? Well, SAP Commerce Cloud merchandising capabilities just got even better with the addition of the Product Image Designer. The AI Image Generation tool lets you automatically create captivating new product images from simple text descriptions. Plus, the Background Editor allows you to transform or remove existing product image backgrounds, staying on-brand and boosting engagement with visuals tailored for commerce catalogs.

These enhancements are designed to make your life easier, letting you focus on what you do best while ensuring your product images look top-notch!

Product Image Designer

 

Adaptive

Boosting resiliency through enhanced cloud operations

SAP Commerce Cloud provides global hosting on Azure, where the platform can be hosted in your region of choice. While each region is divided into multiple availability zones, what happens if one availability zone has a disruption? This is where zone redundancies and Premium Disaster Recovery come in.

The perks of zone redundancies include bolstered resilience and security for your data centers, greater reliability for clusters, and increased availability for critical workloads such as search and storefront operations.

In Q2, we rolled out zone redundancies for Azure SQL databases. This feature automatically replicates databases across different zones within the same region, ensuring that a disruption won’t jeopardize your production environment.

For Q3, we’ve added zone redundancies for the SAP Commerce Cloud application along with key infrastructure, such as compute and load balancers. Keep an eye out for zone redundancies on storage which is coming soon!

We are thrilled to also announce that Premium Disaster Recovery is now available for self-service purchase in SAP Commerce Cloud through your Consumption Credits, a "virtual currency" we introduced earlier this year. You can log in to your cloud portal and assess this feature’s applicability in your enterprise through the updated estimator tool. From there, you can subsequently schedule and manage your upgrade from Standard to Premium Disaster Recovery.

Our systems are already designed with full backup capability in case of a major disruption. If a black swan event were to occur, Premium Disaster Recovery dramatically reduces both the Recovery Point Objective (RPO) and Recovery Time Objective (RTO). In a nutshell, RPO is the amount of data that would be lost, while RTO is the length of time it takes to recover and fully be back online. With Premium Disaster Recovery, the data loss and maximum amount of time to be back to regular online operations are both reduced by at least 75%.

These improvements aim to give you greater control and peace of mind, ensuring your business can bounce back swiftly from any disruptions.

Generate shareable links at a click of a button with Backoffice Deep Linking

Last year, we introduced Backoffice deep linking, and we're excited to take it up a notch with automated link generation. Now, you can easily generate URLs to bookmark and navigate directly to specific pages and items within Backoffice.

Share these links effortlessly with your team, agencies, and other colleagues to boost productivity and collaboration. This update will save you the hassle of manually navigating to specific pages every time. Enjoy a smoother, more efficient workflow!

Improve customer service and site security with Composable Storefront enhancements

Our Composable Storefront now comes with exciting new features designed to elevate customer service and enhance site security.

The Assisted Service Module has been updated to help your customer service agents shine. With just one click, agents can navigate from the customer list to a selected customer's 360-degree view. Switching back to the customer list to check other details is just as seamless. Additionally, the module now allows agents to search using the order ID and apply site filtering, making it a breeze to view order details and product pages for customers.

On the security front, the Composable Storefront now includes a verification step (think "I am not a robot") during customer registration to bolster site security. This comes with preconfigured support for Google reCAPTCHA v2. This added layer of security prevents brute-force attacks in registration and prevents bots from spamming websites.

These updates are all about making customer interactions smoother and more secure, making it a win-win for everyone!

 

Connected

Get a better handle over your order lifecycle with the latest SAP Order Management Services update

We're excited to introduce an option for synchronous integration between SAP Commerce Cloud and SAP Order Management Services that allow for faster and more efficient order processing, real time visibility and reduced overall operational costs.

To further assist you with managing large orders, we've also launched workflow loops. This feature simplifies orchestration by allowing you to apply sophisticated workflows to an even broader set of order data with significantly fewer steps.

Additionally, we've introduced the ability to include reasons for any Order Activity types, such as the rationale behind a delivery failure. This functionality is available for all standard and custom Order Activity types. Reason analysis is a crucial step towards optimizing the order lifecycle, and we're here to support you in achieving that goal.

Loop Control workflow for SAP Order Management Services

Harness AI to optimize marketplace management across 950+ sales channels

The SAP Store has expanded with new innovations and services, enriching our digital experience portfolio. We're thrilled to spotlight our partner, ChannelEngine, which empowers customers to connect and sell products on over 950 different online sales channels and international marketplaces. This solution is designed to maximize conversion rates, increase average order value, and boost customer engagement.

ChannelEngine provides brands, retailers, and distributors with the advanced technical capabilities needed to navigate the complexities of commerce management. By combining the strengths of SAP OMS and ChannelEngine, you can operate commerce unbounded across the globe, with centralized shopper order processing and access to rich insights into consumer demand, sales performance, and market trends. This partnership helps enhance unified commerce decision-making, making your business smarter and more agile.

ChannelEngine connects with 950+ online sales channels

 For more details on the above release features, check out the following resources:

  • Q3 2024 SAP Commerce Cloud Release Readiness Kit
  • Performance Optimization Best Practices
  • SAP Commerce Cloud update cycle
  • SAP Commerce Cloud Release Navigator single access point to release content

SAP Help Portal

  • SAP Commerce Cloud What’s New

Please share your feedback in a comment and follow our SAP Commerce Cloud topic page to connect with the community!

entry.title: Simplified invoices in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition through a SOAP service?
entry.content: I am looking for an equivalent to the SAP Business ByDesign web service "PointOfSaleTransactionProcessingPointOfSaleTransactionIn" in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. I found the following: "Retail Merchandise Management - Point Of Sale Transaction Data Integration (SAP_COM_0652)", but it is not selectable in the Communication Arrangements app. Is there any alternative to access this functionality?

entry.title: Re: How Do I Copy Master Data Fields to a Sales Document?
entry.content: @CE6 I was able to achieve the custom field of copying from Customer Mater to Sales Order but then the value is getting passed but not the description component, description component still stays empty. Do you have any idea as to why this is happening?

entry.title: Re: ADRC table getting updated sometimes when changing the delivery address in ME23N
entry.content:
Hi We are facing the same issue, does anyone know how they fixed it .

It would be a great help

Thanks

entry.title: Transaction code updated as MB**, even though they are obsolete
entry.content:
Transaction code updated as MB** for the material document posted through BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE / IDOC


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Transaction code updated as MB**, even though they ar...
entry.content:
Kindly check the below Documented KBA:
2304535 - BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE - GMCODE for MIGO in table T158G - SAP ERP & S/4 HANA
..............
The entries in table T158G are just program-internal keys, which stay in place, even after the MB transactions become obsolete. The BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE does not call any transaction.
..............

So, this is just an internal naming and standard system design.

entry.title: How can you model a Parent-Child Hierarchy with two Key attributes in the Datasphere?
entry.content:
Hello,

My issue is that I have two dimensional tables: Project and Costs. Each project has one or multiple cost codes.

Project

Pr_nrattributes
001x
002xx
003xxx

and Cost

Pr_nrCost_CodeDepBudget
001AFI10000
001BCO5000
002BFI8000

My problem is that some attributes can only be identified by the combined key of Cost_Code and Pr_nr. I am a beginner in this field, so my expertise is limited. I considered merging the dimensions and using the combined key as the identifier, but my problem still stands. Due to the combined key, I need to build a parent-child hierarchy.

Any help is much appreciated. Thank you in advance.

Best regards,

Marco

entry.title: Re: How to disable the save button in print preview of adobe form

I need to hide the Print and Save push buttons while viewing the spool requests in SP01 for Adobe forms. Kindly suggest. I tried passing SFPOUTPUTPARAMS-NOPRINT = 'X'. But this did not work. Kindly suggest.

entry.title: SAP Datashere - How to flip sign the Measure value by GL Account type like SAP Analytics Cloud.
entry.content:
Before we start discussing, I would like to let you know that you can get a 30-day trial instance of SAP Datasphere and access basic SAP Datasphere trainings here.  https://developers.sap.com/tutorial-navigator.html

As an SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) consultant, I am currently working for a customer who wants to use SAP Datasphere as a reporting data source for SAC. This will allow them to integrate SAC Planning data, Group Reporting Consolidated Actual data, and third-party data.

This is a common scenario, but the main problem is customer cannot figure out how to flip sign based on the GL Account type in SAP Datasphere. Additionally, GL Accounts usually have a hierarchical structure like Balance Sheet (B/S) and Profit & Loss Statement (P/L). Therefore, after the value is aggregated, it should be displayed with the correct sign.

In financial systems, Income and Liability accounts data are usually stored in the database with opposite signs, while Expense and Asset accounts data are stored as they are.

For example, if Revenue is 1000 and Expense is 300, the Net Revenue will be 700.

To achieve this, Revenue is stored as -1000 and Expense as 300. When added to together, the result is -700. Since the Net Revenue account is an Income Account, the Financial system will flip its sign before displaying it to users, showing Net Revenue as 700. 

This behavior is consistent across all SAP systems (ERP, BW, BPC and SAC.) 

Unfortunately, DataSphere does not yet provide this feature. I believe the SAP Datasphere development team is considering it, but we need a solution for financial reporting in the meantime.

The solution is....

'Create a calculated measure in the Analytic Model using a CASE WHEN Statement'.

Let's Assume, we have an attribute in the ACCT dimension called Actype, which has values 'INC', 'EXP,' AST and 'LEQ'. The measure name is Amount.

By creating a calculated measure called 'CM SIGNDATA' as shown below, we can display the correct sign data. The Amount measure shows the same sign as it is stored in the database.

CASE
    WHEN Actype_ACCT = 'INC’ THEN Amount * (-1)
    WHEN Actype_ACCT = 'LEQ’ THEN Amount * (-1)
    ELSE
          Amount
END

I hope SAP Datasphere supports this as built-in feature like SAC so that we don't need to create additional calculated Measure.

NOTE:  you can find Actype_ACCT attribute when you create the calculated measure. After selecting 'Dimension' and searching 'ACCT', you will find it. just click it then it will be inserted in the formula without typing.

I hope this article helps anyone facing similar issues.

entry.title: Re: How to connect the QR-code reader for processing the entry invoices (Swiss legal requirement)
entry.content: comment made by mistake, added tags

entry.title: SAP Ariba Buying 360
entry.content:
Could you please advise what needs to be done to enable Buying 360 for Ariba Buying customer? I see there are some relevant parameters available but i guess thats not all that must be done. 

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: CPI Message Mapping Issue with Simulate
entry.content:
Remove the xmlns & targetNamespace attributes in the root xsd:schema tag (you can also remove elementFormDefault & attributeFormDefault) because that is causing the message mapping to expect all xml tags in your payload to have the ns1 prefix which is why the map doesn't produce the desired output.  Changing elementFormDefault to "unqualified" almost solves the problem, but the mapping still expects the root tag to include the prefix which does not exist in your payload.

 

Regards,

Ryan Crosby

entry.title: How to integrate SAP Datasphere Consumption API in SAP BTP Destination and SAP BTP-BPA
entry.content:
Hi All,

I am facing an issue when creating a destination on Sap data sphere Consumption API. I am getting a 401 authorization issue when creating a Destination using Oauth 2.0. However, using the same URL in Postman is working fine.

Can you suggest a solution to this issue?

This is the authorization I am using for Postman.

entry.title: Same profile naming in development and quality systems
entry.content:
Hi,
SHD an SHQ were our development and quality systems, but now, we have a new system:
SDH (development system)
SQH (quality System)
So, when we created new role and a new profile is assigned automatically in old systems, this profile name was T-SDxxxxxx (DEV) and T-SQxxx (QAS) respectively.
Now, when we create new roles and profiles automatically in new systems, the profile name begins with same nomenclature T-SHxxxx and it is generating incidents.
Could you please help us with this issue?
Is these any OSS Note to apply? or could you tell us how change the beginning of the profile name T-YYxxxxx?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Same profile naming in development and quality systems
entry.content:
This behaviour can be changed by creating a new entry in table USR_CUST.

Parameter PRGN_PROF_PREFIX controls the naming convention of profiles generated by PFCG.

You can check SAP Note 0001380203 - FAQ | PFCG: Naming patterns for generated profile names.

This documentation has a full description of the scenarios and it's applications.

Be advised, that SAP always recommend using a single system in a landscape for User and Role maintenance to prevent this exact scenario from happening.

entry.title: Re: Usage of "JustAsk" within the story in a separate panel.

entry.title: CCM Type - 0SF1 - Sales Rebate Fixed Amount, Delta Accrual posting error for Profit center
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I've created a CC of type - 0SF1 - Sales Rebate with Fixed Amount and maintained a Settlement Material (which has a Profit Center assigned in the material master). When I tried to post the Delta Accrual for the same CC, system is not able to determine Profit Center from this settlement material in the Delta Accrual document.

Please let me know if I'm missing any settings in CCM config which is causing this issue.

Profit Center is Missing in Account Assignment

Regards,
Sudip

entry.title: Avoid PIR While creating 3Party sales order for confirmed date
entry.content:
When creating a third party sales order (item category TAS) initially at the time of creation the confirmed date of the schedule line determines based on the  Purchase Info Record (planned Delivery time). This is not the realistic date.

Now after creation of purchase order - once the vendor acknowledges the PO, the AB line gets updated in the purchase order confirmations tab. Now this Date is updated as the confirmed delivery date in the sales order. This is the realistic date.

Now business want to restrict the confirmed date happening via the purchase information record. This is because to send the realistic date as order acknowledgment to customer.

Can we avoid determination of initial confirmed delivery date which is happening via PIR ?

Thanks,

Anusha Katta

entry.title: Re: S4Hana VS SAP R3
entry.content: Simple question, simple answer: R3 - old, S/4HANA - new ;))

entry.title: Check out the updated SAP BTP Developer's Guide and let us know what you think
entry.content:
Last year on TechEd the SAP BTP Developer's Guide was announced as part of the SAP BTP Guidance Framework initiative. The goal of this guide was to give the developers building applications on SAP Business Technology Platform a recommended path they can safely follow. 

This year, keeping this promise, we've improved the SAP BTP Developer's guide adapting its structure to the design-led development process. We've brought more content for developers and development architects and leads and we will keep on working on it.

To help us bring the best content to you, go and start using the guide. Then, fill in the Survey: Help us Improve the SAP BTP Developer's Guide! 

You can also use the Feedback option of the SAP Help Portal providing the following options:

  • Log in and leave comments in the SAP Help Portal directly
  • Create an issue in GitHub for a particular page
  • Create a pull request again in GitHub 

Enjoy the SAP BTP Developer's Guide!

entry.title: Re: Activation Material Ledger Issue
entry.content:
Hello, 

 

I am not sure if you were able to fix this issue, but I actually ran into what appears to be the exact same issue. 

For us, the cause was incorrect values put into table TCKM2 under MATLED column.  

 

Plants DL06 and SD06 were assigned invalid material ledger values of 0000. This value should have been 9000 in our case, as 0000 was not defined in OMX2. (I have no idea how they managed to set it to 0000) 

I had tried to update the value from the database, but it didn't work. 

Eventually, we were able to manually change these values by loading the table TCKM2 in se11 through debug mode. This allowed us to change the assignment without using OMX3. However, this could cause more damage to the system if you aren't familiar with this you should probably ask SAP Support.

entry.title: Avoid PIR when creating third party sales order when schedulling
entry.content:
When creating a third party sales order (item category TAS) initially at the time of creation the confirmed date of the schedule line determines based on the  Purchase Info Record (planned Delivery time). This is not the realistic date.

Now after creation of purchase order - once the vendor acknowledges the PO, the AB line gets updated in the purchase order confirmations tab. Now this Date is updated as the confirmed delivery date in the sales order. This is the realistic date.

Now business want to restrict the confirmed date happening via the purchase information record. This is because to send the realistic date as order acknowledgment to customer.

Can we avoid determination of initial confirmed delivery date which is happening via PIR ?

This is for S/4 HANA Public Cloud 2408

entry.title: Re: SAP Single Sign-On: Authenticate with Kerberos/SPNEGO
entry.content:
Hello Martina,

My client, for obvious audit reasons, is looking to implement an SAP SSO solution within an SAP ECC 6.0 EHP4 SP16 landscape based on NetWeaver 7.01 SP16. I know it’s quite outdated—they’ve postponed the upgrade deadline…

The end users are using the SAP GUI client. User accounts are provisioned through a CUA installed on Solution Manager 7.2 SPS18 (NW 7.4 SP30).

The client has shared additional constraints regarding their infrastructure: “The workstations are not joined to a traditional Active Directory domain but are instead integrated with Azure Active Directory (EntraID), making an SNC/Kerberos-based SSO solution not the best option for this context. Additionally, their environment uses Cloud Sync rather than Azure AD Connect.”

I also reviewed the SSO3 PAM document: "PAM_SSO_30.pdf." On page 12, “SAP Single Sign-On 3.0 Authentication Type Support for SAP NetWeaver Versions” indicates that the SAML protocol, as well as web clients, are not applicable—no service provider for NW 7.01.

I found note 3314365 - SSO with Azure AD for SAP GUI support, which suggests two technical solutions:

  • SAML with Java NetWeaver Server
  • Secure Login Service

Ideally, I would have chosen SAP SSO3/SNC with a Kerberos token for an SAP GUI client.

The Secure Login Client is supported by Azure AD and on-premise AD for Kerberos token-based SSO (note 3245617 - SAP SSO Fixes for Secure Login Client 3.0 SP 02 Patch 14).

But how can SAP SSO3 with SNC & Kerberos be integrated if the IdP is Azure AD? Is it impossible, or feasible but unsupported by SAP at any point? Should I suggest that the client upgrade their ERP ASAP since SSO is no longer applicable with modern technologies?

Could you please advise on which SAP SSO solution I can implement with our SAP ECC landscape?

Best regards.

entry.title: Re: Introducing SAP Analytics Cloud, add-in for Microsoft PowerPoint
entry.content:
Hi @JanKuboschek1 ,

Thanks for sharing the feedback. 

Once you are able to sign-in successfully, you will be able to see the Explore and Favourites page in Add-in and all other buttons (Refresh, Sign-out) will be enabled. if you are still observing this issue kindly create an Incident and we will help you on this. 

Note: Currently Add-in is supported in PPT web version only and Desktop is planned in upcoming releases. 

Roadmap Explorer for planned features is work in progress and we will update all the planned features. 

Regards,
Ashok R

entry.title: Re: CPI Message Mapping Issue with Simulate
entry.content: @Supreet_Sekhon the schemaLocation attribute in the root tag AgileData is fouling up the execution of the mapping. Simulation is successful with the updates that I have mentioned and the removal of that one attribute at the root (it's causing the system to try and read that schema during execution I believe).

entry.title: Re: Exposing as a V2 Odata Service in SAP CAP
entry.content:
Hello, did you deployed this application? Because when deployed, fiori app can't find the odata/v2/myservice/$metadata

i tried adding server.js as mentioned from the package's github page, but it didn't worked either

"use strict";
const cds = require("@sap/cds");
const proxy = require("@cap-js-community/odata-v2-adapter");
cds.on("bootstrap", app => app.use(proxy()));
module.exports = cds.server;

there is a section 'Cloud Foundry Deployment' in package's github page but i couldn't find an example for that, it works fine on locale

entry.title: Editable DataTable Issue on List Picker
entry.content:
Hi,

we have an issue using Editable Data Table.

Changing a field value the values of others fields / list picker get resetted.

See this video:

Code of the list picker inside the Data Table object:

{
    "DisplayType": "Text",
    "EditType": "List",
    "NumberOfLines": 1,
    "TextAlignment": "Left",
    "ListPicker": {
        "PickerItems": {
            "Target": {
                "Service": "/App/Services/ServiceV2.service",
                "EntitySet": "PosTypeVHSet",
                "ServerSidePaging": true
            },
            "DisplayValue": "{PosType} {Description}",
            "ReturnValue": "{PosType}"
        },
        "Value": "{TypePos}"
    }
},

Another minor issue is that the OnPress event of the DataTable works only when Data Table has EditMode = 'None'. For example if I click on a not editable cell the OnPress event is not triggered. Instead, if I set DataTable EditMode to 'None', the OnPress event is triggered but if I try to get the binding of the selected item, i get instead the page binding.

I'm using latest MDK V24.7.3 and I work on Android 14.0

Am I doing something wrong ?

Thank you.

entry.title: Exception in line 401 of include LRSA3U03 (program SAPLRSA3) UNCAUGHT_EXCEPTION occurred in line 1
entry.content:
We use odata to load data to both SAP BW as well as Amazon Web Services (AWS). As we attempt to initialize an AWS odata service, we are getting the following error messages:

Exception occurred in line 401 of include LRSA3U03 (program SAPLRSA3)
Exception UNCAUGHT_EXCEPTION occurred in line 1 of include LRSA3U03 (program SAPLRSA3)
Error with extractor call RSC1_INIT_BIW_GET
Exception occurred in line 140 of include CL_RODPS_DATASOURCE_CURSOR====CM00D (program CL_RODPS_DATASOURCE_CURSOR====CP)
Error while accessing
Exception occurred in line 241 of include CL_RODPS_DATASOURCE_CURSOR====CM009 (program CL_RODPS_DATASOURCE_CURSOR====CP)

We were able to execute a full load of all of the data the prior day but have not been able to establish an initialization run.

Any assistance with how to resolve this error is greatly appreciated.

Kind Regards,

Ann

entry.title: Adobe form hyperlink
entry.content:
Hi ABAPers,

I am unable to create hyperlink in adobe forms , taking text from SO10.

Used smartstyles method <HL>, used <a href > method , made the change in text field to rich text - Not worked.

My requirement is I have text in so10 and wanted to make one string as hyperlink

Any solution for this?

entry.title: Re: ADT ABAP Debugger - What to do if your program does not stop at breakpoints
entry.content:
@Armin_Beilthank you for sharing this, it's a very helpful list!

I know this is an old post (from 2020!) but it comes up as a top result when searching for "why breakpoint does not work", so I'm just adding one item we found. We ran into an issue with debugging a BAdI where none of the tricks worked other than the endless loop one. It turned out BAdI was run in update process and debugger behaved differently than we used to. The steps below are for SAP GUI debugger but probably are similar in ADT.

Even with Update Debugging switched on, it ignores the breakpoints that were set before update started. In order to debug update process, you must first get into it, and then you can use the breakpoints. These are the steps in SAP GUI debugger.

  1. Make sure you have 2 extra sessions available. Get into the position where you're ready to debug and start the debugger (/h).
  2. When you're in debugger, make sure Update Debugging is checked in your Settings (in the menu).
  3. Then just hit Continue and wait until you get into update process. You might get some messages or additional pop-ups in the regular screen if that's part of the process, don't panic! Eventually a new session will open with "update" in the screen title.
  4. Now click big red Create Breakpoint (F9) button. From there, enter where you want to place the breakpoint. For a BAdI, enter class and method.
  5. After that, hit Continue and it will stop at the breakpoint.

    The key is you must get into debugger first and get into the update process.

entry.title: Unable to create hyperlink in adobe form from SO10 Text
entry.content:
Hi ABAPers,

I need to create hyperlink for adobe form , when I am executing adobe form seperately  it isworking , but when I am trying to print by giving quatation no it is not printling as a hyperlink.

Any solution for this?

entry.title: Can I release 0FI_ACDOCA_10 for ODP myself?
entry.content:
I want to extract data to a data lake using the SAP CDC connector in Azure Data Factory. I have successfully extracted from 0FI_GL_14 and other S-API sources. However, in our S4, in transaction RSA5 the extractor 0FI_ACDOCA_10 and 20 are not released for ODP.


Based on previous posts, it should be possible to have them released for ODP, and based on one conversation, I should be able to do it. On the other hand, some documentation says RODPS_OS_EXPOSE is only for customer written extractors. 

So can I make the extractor available for ODP, or is the extractor just not available with our version and SPS level? (S/4HANA OP, SAP_BASIS 756, SAP_ABA 75G, SAP_BW 756, SPS 000 for all of those)

At least I'm not able to select the extractor for releasing - list appears empty.

entry.title: Re: Error "FAIL: process disp+work Dispatcher not running" when running docker image
entry.content:
Same error and I also made a change to the DEFAULT profile via RZ10. 
I'm trying to recover from this. Did you manage to restore a working profile?

entry.title: Re: First sync SAP MDK timeout
entry.content: When MDK is binding to entity sets it will use data paging by default.  The default is 50 records at a time.  However, if you are referring to offline initialization then the MDK paging does not apply.  For offline initialization, the request will be based on the defining request data and typically the odata server will handles the paging of the result set back via server side paging.

entry.title: Re: After simulation, try out validation. Identity Provisioning closes the loop with a fresh new tes

entry.content:
Calangute "Goa "Call "girl+91 7206600611 Service
